name: Val-Cit-PABC
trigger_detail: Lysosomal cathepsin B cleavage of Cit-PABC amide bond
description: Gold-standard enzymatically cleavable dipeptide linker. Val-Cit sequence is specifically recognized and cleaved by cathepsin B in lysosomes. PABC acts as a self-immolative spacer.

name: Mc-Val-Cit-PABC
description: Full ADC linker-payload connector. Maleimide (Mc) attaches to antibody cysteine thiols via Michael addition. Val-Cit-PABC provides enzymatic cleavage.

name: Hydrazone simple
description: Classic acid-labile hydrazone linker. C=N bond stable at neutral pH but hydrolyzes rapidly in acidic environments. First-gen ADC linker with plasma stability concerns.

name: Hydrazone benzyl
description: Sterically hindered hydrazone with benzyl group. Slightly more stable than simple hydrazone due to steric protection.

name: Acetal linker
description: Acid-labile acetal linker. Very stable at pH 7.4 (t1/2 > 24h) but hydrolyzes within minutes at pH 5.0. Suitable for fast lysosomal payload release.

name: Carbonate ester
description: pH-sensitive carbonate linker. Hydrolyzes via acid-catalyzed or esterase-mediated mechanism. More stable than hydrazone at neutral pH.

name: Ketal linker
description: Ketal-based acid-labile linker. Similar to acetal but with additional alkyl substitution for tunable stability.

name: Disulfide SPDP
description: Reductively cleavable disulfide linker. Stable in bloodstream (low GSH ~5 μM) but reduced in cytoplasm (high GSH 1-10 mM). Steric hindrance near disulfide can tune cleavage rate.

name: Disulfide hindered
description: Sterically hindered disulfide linker. Slower reduction kinetics for more controlled payload release.

name: Non-cleavable SMCC
description: Complete antibody degradation in lysosome required
drugs_using: Non-cleavable linker using SMCC. Requires complete proteolytic degradation of antibody to release payload with attached linker residue. Often retains activity despite modification.

name: TMALIN-like dual
description: Inspired by Yili Bio's TMALIN platform. Dual cleavage: first at tumor microenv pH to expose membrane-permeable payload then complete release in lysosome. Supports DAR=8 with high homogeneity.

name: Carbamate PABC
trigger_detail: Acid-catalyzed carbamate decomposition releasing CO2 + amine
description: Carbamate-based self-immolative spacer. Commonly used in PABC-type linkers. Releases payload via 1

name: Ester labile
description: Simple ester-based cleavable linker. Tunable hydrolysis rate by modifying steric bulk near ester. Less plasma-stable than carbamate.

name: Imine Schiff base
description: Imine-based pH-responsive linker. Faster hydrolysis kinetics than hydrazone but lower plasma stability. Can be stabilized by adjacent aromatic groups.

name: Silyl ether
description: Silyl ether linker with tunable stability based on silicon substituents. Less common in ADCs but useful for specific applications.

name: PEG spacer
description: Non-cleavable PEG spacer for improving solubility
drugs_using: PEG (polyethylene glycol) spacer used to increase linker hydrophilicity and reduce aggregation. Not cleavable but improves pharmacokinetics.

name: GSH-responsive disulfide
description: Simple glutathione-responsive disulfide. Used as a model system for studying redox-cleavable linker kinetics.
